market_news.title: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) Global S&P Futures lower in premarket
market_news.content: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) is experiencing lower global S&P Futures in premarket trading. The report also notes mixed performance for the Nikkei and DAX, with WTI Crude oil at $91, natural gas up 1.5%, and gold at $4408. The overall analyst rating for SPY is currently a "SELL."

items.title: BNP Paribas Adjusts Eaton Price Target to $460 From $440, Maintains Outperform Rating
items.content: BNP Paribas has increased its price target for Eaton Corporation to $460 from $440, while maintaining an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects the firm's positive outlook on Eaton's potential, despite the specific reasons for the target raise not being detailed in this brief report. The article also mentions Eaton's collaboration with Nvidia on the Eaton Beam Rubin DSX platform for the data center market.

items.title: Triumph Capital Management Has $293,000 Stock Holdings in International Business Machines Corporation $IBM

Despite this, major institutions like Vanguard and State Street remain heavily invested, contributing to IBM's 58.96% institutional ownership. items.title: Broadcom exec says Taiwan Semiconductor is reaching capacity limits - report
items.content: A Broadcom executive reported that Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), their manufacturing partner, is reaching its production capacity limits. This issue is attributed to broader supply chain constraints within the industry, driven by the escalating demand for AI chips. The statement highlights a potential bottleneck in the production of crucial components for artificial intelligence.

items.title: Global X Semiconductor ETF Guide (ASX: SEMI): What Is It All About?
items.content: The Global X Semiconductor ETF (ASX: SEMI) offers Australian investors concentrated exposure to global semiconductor companies, tracking the Solactive Global Semiconductor 30 Index. While it provides a direct way to invest in this critical sector and is easy to buy on the ASX, it is unhedged against currency fluctuations and represents a high-conviction, concentrated thematic holding rather than a diversified core portfolio component. items.title: OpenAI flags Microsoft dependence as key business risk ahead of expected IPO - report
items.content: OpenAI has reportedly identified its significant dependence on Microsoft as a key business risk in a document resembling an IPO prospectus. The company stated that Microsoft provides a substantial portion of its financing and computing resources. OpenAI warned that any alteration or termination of this partnership, or its inability to diversify partners, could negatively impact its business, financial condition, and operating results.

items.title: Astera Labs Conference: AI Infrastructure Shift Boosts Scorpio Switches, Taurus Cables and Retimers
items.content: Astera Labs highlights how the evolving AI infrastructure, with its demand for higher speeds and larger cluster sizes, is increasing the need for its connectivity solutions. The company's Scorpio PCIe switches, Taurus active electrical cables, and Aries retimers are experiencing boosted demand as AI systems scale, addressing critical speed and distance bottlenecks. Astera Labs is also expanding its product footprint into optical solutions and NVLink Fusion, anticipating further growth in these a

items.title: How Ford is using AI to power its next-gen ambitions
items.content: Ford Motor is aggressively integrating artificial intelligence across its engineering, software, and enterprise systems to transform into a software-led mobility company. This initiative, driven by its EPEO organization, aims to accelerate processes like engineering simulations and enhance productivity, with India serving as a growing hub for strategic AI and platform development. The move is crucial for Ford as it navigates rising EV losses and aims to compete with new electric vehicle entrants

items.title: Apple Maps To Get Ads Soon
items.content: Apple is reportedly planning to introduce local advertising within its Apple Maps app, potentially as soon as this month, to generate more revenue from its services. Similar to Google Maps, this system would allow advertisers to bid for ad slots based on search queries, with the highest-paying local businesses being displayed first. This move is expected to operate similarly to Apple's App Store ads.

items.title: Domino's® Updates Its Iconic, Industry-First Tracker for an Even Better Customer Experience
items.content: Domino's has updated its iconic, industry-first Tracker with new enhancements aimed at improving the customer experience. The updated Tracker features a more precise ready time using AI technology, Live Activities for iOS users, and a redesigned interface with clearer, more detailed progress views. These changes aim to make order tracking easier, faster, and more intuitive for customers from placement to the first bite.

items.title: Johnson & Johnson Stock Hands $160 Bil Back – Worth a Look?
items.content: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) has returned $160 billion to shareholders over the last decade through dividends and buybacks, making it the 8th highest in history for shareholder returns. While these returns signify financial health, the article questions if high capital returns might sometimes sacrifice growth, comparing JNJ's conservative growth with faster-growing tech companies. Despite solid fundamentals, JNJ stock is not immune to significant market downturns.

items.title: Steve Wozniak Talks Apple @50, AI, and Big Tech
items.content: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak discussed Apple's 50th anniversary, the growth of AI, and concerns about Big Tech's increasing influence during an appearance on 'The Claman Countdown'. Wozniak's insights cover the historical milestone of Apple and his perspectives on current technological trends and corporate power.

items.title: Western Digital Corp stock faces AI-driven demand surge amid NAND flash pricing volatility
items.content: Western Digital Corp reported strong Q2 2026 results with 15% revenue growth driven by AI data center demand for NAND and HDD storage, beating analyst expectations. However, investors face concerns about NAND pricing volatility due to industry oversupply, even as the company pivots to AI-optimized storage and projects significant AI-related revenue growth. The stock's performance reflects the sector's sensitivity to hyperscaler spending and cyclical risks in NAND pricing.
